## kurai      -0.026135866 0.1690948 -0.358613777  0.305169049
## kusai      -0.094954731 0.1724503 -0.433691925  0.242041453
## machigau    0.243611428 0.1657054 -0.078749725  0.571577955
## majimena    0.144078917 0.1593970 -0.167348698  0.458934756
## mamoru      0.212820264 0.1611444 -0.100806144  0.530835592
## marena      0.100413823 0.1678421 -0.229046113  0.431451888
## marui       0.335526100 0.1591251  0.026654127  0.649271155
## matomeru    0.378319352 0.1675203  0.054904168  0.710994535
## matsuri     0.288218523 0.1570153 -0.019061325  0.599405698
## mijikana    0.075658293 0.1696208 -0.255203734  0.411559651
## miseru      0.401546391 0.1759817  0.061143351  0.752406501
## mitsumeru  -0.133365224 0.1522884 -0.433898558  0.164138809
## mizu        0.439728784 0.1634082  0.122367655  0.764624100
## moji        0.151626544 0.1718759 -0.184075540  0.488113607
## mukashi     0.332117946 0.1618453  0.014030804  0.651231198
## nakama      0.148943326 0.1587913 -0.163172660  0.458803298
## namida     -0.102082286 0.1682854 -0.433884420  0.228012744
## naritatsu  -0.073326748 0.1592131 -0.386931209  0.237049422
## nemui       0.200560935 0.1671428 -0.125429742  0.529377091
## nigatena    0.238497080 0.1512186 -0.055917574  0.535700993
## noberu     -0.113102328 0.1645630 -0.437828791  0.208157193
## noru       -0.176917409 0.1597529 -0.491429802  0.133391616
## nosori      0.445107179 0.1589328  0.140545059  0.760157188
## nukeru      0.084402557 0.1622034 -0.235325118  0.404309437
## pekon      -0.148086372 0.1714038 -0.488449625  0.186166949
## petan       0.073409678 0.1695042 -0.260559351  0.405233322
## pikari     -0.148262860 0.1559474 -0.456553552  0.151616194
## sadameru    0.004188142 0.1583836 -0.305529131  0.311923110
## sakana      0.197152700 0.1665619 -0.131133215  0.529318952
## samui       0.132842914 0.1609262 -0.184262864  0.449470586
## shinu       0.144190685 0.1674279 -0.184487451  0.473255993
## shiraberu   0.129529341 0.1674074 -0.196801436  0.457137075
## shiroi      0.122951243 0.1621935 -0.195553605  0.441461480
## shobon     -0.331236561 0.1604380 -0.650485493 -0.020910445
## takumina    0.527126682 0.1656517  0.205524395  0.855039135
## tegami      0.174877816 0.1645516 -0.146601823  0.497376065
## tegaruna   -0.062679173 0.1686389 -0.394106610  0.264656519
## toboshii    0.311334829 0.1608219 -0.003526065  0.627802046
## tobu        0.420060520 0.1684451  0.092549861  0.754563477
## tochi       0.120680264 0.1562094 -0.184647256  0.427815876
## tomonau     0.239371558 0.1535937 -0.060623711  0.543284453
## tsubuyaku   0.213345836 0.1681405 -0.112845262  0.547452758
## tsuma       0.222448250 0.1562240 -0.081928653  0.528191681
## tsuratsura  0.472880528 0.1652800  0.154243671  0.804076482
## tsuyoi      0.340126910 0.1655916  0.018395020  0.669058035
## wasawasa    0.090724586 0.1569102 -0.217132363  0.398845617
## wasureru    0.221094320 0.1529705 -0.075075392  0.521747785
## yobu        0.355479246 0.1654601  0.031424496  0.680545413
## yomu        0.389988462 0.1723216  0.053891848  0.731514914
## yuku        0.519379664 0.1681359  0.196536388  0.853594016
## yume        0.247643886 0.1549860 -0.055890322  0.552394426
## yutakana   -0.345955995 0.1696201 -0.681617864 -0.016217923

Make this into a tibble and make the row names into a column that we can index:

word_posts <- word_posts |>
  as.data.frame() |> 
  rownames_to_column('word')

Add the word class information back into there, as well as from the items tibble the iconicity quantile:

word_posts <- word_posts |>
  left_join(distinct(E1, word, word_class),
            by = c('word' = 'word')) |> 
  relocate(word_class, .after = word) |> 
  left_join(distinct(items, romaji, quantile),
            by = c('word' = 'romaji')) |> 
  relocate(quantile, .after = word_class)

We’ll also want to plot descriptive means to make sure the picture isn’t tainted too much by shrinkage / so as to not to artificially deflate variation and suggest that results are more consistent as they are.

word_posts <- left_join(word_posts, acc_item_avgs_both,
                        by = c('word' = 'word'))

Sort the tibble by Estimate (posterior mean accuracy) and code the word factor with the levels in that order so that we don’t have to use reorder() for the ggplot2 below:

word_posts <- word_posts |> arrange(Estimate)

# Factor code:

word_posts <- word_posts |> 
  mutate(word = factor(word, levels = word_posts$word))

It will be more intuitive for most audiences to plot this in probability space.

word_posts <- word_posts |> 
  mutate(estimate = plogis(Estimate),
         lower_ci = plogis(Q2.5),
         upper_ci = plogis(Q97.5))

4.7.2 Plot sequence for Evolang presentation

Make a plot of the posterior draws first:

# Plot core:

word_p <- word_posts |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = word, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_segment(aes(y = lower_ci, yend = upper_ci)) +
  geom_hline(linetype = 'dashed', yintercept = 0.5)

# Axes and cosmetics:

word_p <- word_p +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.02, 0.02)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.0),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                     expand = c(0, 0),
                     labels = str_c(seq(0, 100, 20), '%')) +
  xlab(NULL) +
  ylab(NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

# Show and save:

word_p

ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/png/word_posteriors.png',
       width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/pdf/word_posteriors.pdf',
       width = 12, height = 5)

Plot the same thing with added descriptive means on there:

# Plot core:

word_p <- word_posts |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = word, y = estimate)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_segment(aes(y = lower_ci, yend = upper_ci)) +
  geom_hline(linetype = 'dashed', yintercept = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(y = acc_mean),
             shape = 23, fill = 'goldenrod1',
             size = 2.5)

# Axes and cosmetics:

word_p <- word_p +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.02, 0.02)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.0),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                     expand = c(0, 0),
                     labels = str_c(seq(0, 100, 20), '%')) +
  xlab(NULL) +
  ylab(NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

# Show and save:

word_p

ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/png/word_posteriors_with_descriptive_means.png',
       width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/pdf/word_posteriors_with_descriptive_means.pdf',
       width = 12, height = 5)

For Evolang presentation, we’ll toggle through this and highlight the different word classes, as well as the highest iconicity quantile (quantile = 5).

Quantile highlight:

# Plot core:

word_p <- word_posts |> 
  mutate(high_quantile = if_else(quantile == '5', 'high iconicity', 'other'),
         high_quantile = factor(high_quantile, levels = c('other', 'high iconicity'))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = word, y = estimate, col = high_quantile)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_segment(aes(y = lower_ci, yend = upper_ci)) +
  geom_hline(linetype = 'dashed', yintercept = 0.5)

# Axes and cosmetics:

word_p <- word_p +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('gray90', 'darkorchid3')) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.02, 0.02)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.0),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                     expand = c(0, 0),
                     labels = str_c(seq(0, 100, 20), '%')) +
  xlab(NULL) +
  ylab(NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = 'none')

# Show and save:

word_p

ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/png/word_posteriors_with_quantile5.png',
       width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/pdf/word_posteriors_with_quantile5.pdf',
       width = 12, height = 5)

Ideophone highlight:

# Plot core:

word_p <- word_posts |> 
  mutate(class_red = if_else(word_class == 'Mim', 'mimetic', 'other'),
         class_red = factor(class_red, levels = c('other', 'mimetic'))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = word, y = estimate, col = class_red)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_segment(aes(y = lower_ci, yend = upper_ci)) +
  geom_hline(linetype = 'dashed', yintercept = 0.5)

# Axes and cosmetics:

word_p <- word_p +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('gray90', 'darkorchid3')) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.02, 0.02)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.0),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                     expand = c(0, 0),
                     labels = str_c(seq(0, 100, 20), '%')) +
  xlab(NULL) +
  ylab(NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = 'none')

# Show and save:

word_p

ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/png/word_posteriors_with_mimetics.png',
       width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/pdf/word_posteriors_with_mimetics.pdf',
       width = 12, height = 5)

Verb highlight:

# Plot core:

word_p <- word_posts |> 
  mutate(class_red = if_else(word_class == 'Verb', 'verb', 'other'),
         class_red = factor(class_red, levels = c('other', 'verb'))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = word, y = estimate, col = class_red)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_segment(aes(y = lower_ci, yend = upper_ci)) +
  geom_hline(linetype = 'dashed', yintercept = 0.5)

# Axes and cosmetics:

word_p <- word_p +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('gray90', 'darkorchid3')) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.02, 0.02)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.0),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                     expand = c(0, 0),
                     labels = str_c(seq(0, 100, 20), '%')) +
  xlab(NULL) +
  ylab(NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = 'none')

# Show and save:

word_p

ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/png/word_posteriors_with_verbs.png',
       width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/pdf/word_posteriors_with_verbs.pdf',
       width = 12, height = 5)

Adjective highlight:

# Plot core:

word_p <- word_posts |> 
  mutate(class_red = if_else(word_class == 'Adj', 'adjective', 'other'),
         class_red = factor(class_red, levels = c('other', 'adjective'))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = word, y = estimate, col = class_red)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_segment(aes(y = lower_ci, yend = upper_ci)) +
  geom_hline(linetype = 'dashed', yintercept = 0.5)

# Axes and cosmetics:

word_p <- word_p +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('gray90', 'darkorchid3')) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.02, 0.02)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.0),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                     expand = c(0, 0),
                     labels = str_c(seq(0, 100, 20), '%')) +
  xlab(NULL) +
  ylab(NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = 'none')

# Show and save:

word_p

ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/png/word_posteriors_with_adjectives.png',
       width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/pdf/word_posteriors_with_adjectives.pdf',
       width = 12, height = 5)

Noun highlight:

# Plot core:

word_p <- word_posts |> 
  mutate(class_red = if_else(word_class == 'Noun', 'noun', 'other'),
         class_red = factor(class_red, levels = c('other', 'noun'))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = word, y = estimate, col = class_red)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_segment(aes(y = lower_ci, yend = upper_ci)) +
  geom_hline(linetype = 'dashed', yintercept = 0.5)

# Axes and cosmetics:

word_p <- word_p +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('gray90', 'darkorchid3')) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0.02, 0.02)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.0),
                     breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                     expand = c(0, 0),
                     labels = str_c(seq(0, 100, 20), '%')) +
  xlab(NULL) +
  ylab(NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = 'none')

# Show and save:

word_p

ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/png/word_posteriors_with_nouns.png',
       width = 12, height = 5)
ggsave(plot = word_p, filename = '../figures/pdf/word_posteriors_with_nouns.pdf',
       width = 12, height = 5)

4.8 Visualize posteriors of random effects

Kuleshova et al. (2026) discuss how there is more item variability than subject variability. If there is any signal in the words themselves, this should be the case here too. Let’s make a plot of this:

# Plot core:

sd_p <- both_posts |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = sd_unique_id__Intercept:sd_word__Intercept,
               names_to = 'sd', values_to = 'estimate') |> 
  mutate(sd = if_else(sd == 'sd_unique_id__Intercept',
                      'participant', 'word')) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate)) +
  geom_density(aes(fill = sd), alpha = 0.6)

# Plot core:

sd_p <- sd_p +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c('burlywood3', 'darkorchid4')) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0),
                     limits = c(0, 15)) +
  xlab('Logit estimate') +
  ylab(NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(face = 'bold', margin = margin(t = 6))) +
  theme(axis.line.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = 'none')

sd_p

ggsave('../figures/pdf/experiment_sd_comparisons.pdf',
       width = 6, height = 2.8)
ggsave('../figures/png/experiment_sd_comparisons.png',
       width = 6, height = 2.8)

This completes this analysis.
